spec_match.rationale: Renders the headline value proposition: a button with interestfor=popover-id has identical hover/focus/dismiss behaviour to a hand-wired JS popover, with no script. Spec PR #11006 motivates exactly this comparison.

interactive.rationale: Two side-by-side buttons (manual JS popover vs interestfor) the user can hover and focus to feel the difference; the manual path includes mouseenter/leave/focus/blur handlers visible in source for contrast.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature blurb sells 'zero JS, same behaviour, plus dismiss-on-leave handled by the browser' and the page shows that exact A/B.

edge_cases.rationale: Hover + focus covered. Missing: touch long-press (the third interest signal in the spec), the case where the popover is already :popover-open (re-trigger), and the spec's interest-target-show-delay / interest-target-hide-delay CSS knobs.

references.rationale: Links chromestatus.com/feature/4530756656562176 in the see-also list.

design_system.rationale: Stage buttons use CSS variables; .stage-btn override sets background+color; no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Add touch long-press as a third input mode in the demo
openQuestions.detail: interestfor is meant to respond to every interest signal — mouse hover, keyboard focus, AND touch long-press. The page only demonstrates the first two. On a touch device the long-press path is the most important to show because it's the most novel; add a touch hint banner and an actual long-press handler comparison.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the interest-target-show-delay / hide-delay CSS properties
openQuestions.detail: The spec exposes CSS properties to tune the delays. The sibling delay-tuner concept covers that in depth; the canonical interestfor-attr demo could surface a default-vs-tuned subtle hint so visitors see the delay surface exists.

openQuestions.title: Show the dismiss-on-leave timeline
openQuestions.detail: The page claims 'dismiss-on-leave handled by the browser' — but the user can only verify it by hovering and waiting. Add a small status badge that prints 'interest gained' / 'interest lost' / 'dismissed (xs after leave)' so the browser-managed lifecycle becomes legible.

summary: Clean A/B comparator that lands the headline 'zero JS' story. Extend with touch long-press, expose the delay-tuning surface lightly, and add a lifecycle status readout so the browser-managed behaviour is visible.
